title: Cherry-pick narrowly and record provenance
description: Move a single fix across branches with cherry-pick only when a merge is not appropriate, and use -x so the new commit references its origin.
body: Use when one specific commit must land on another branch ahead of a full merge, such as a hotfix to a release line. Do not cherry-pick a long range that a later merge will also bring over. Actions: pick the minimal commit set; use -x to record the source hash in the message; plan how the divergence resolves so the change is not applied twice. Validation signals include a clear provenance line in the new commit and tests passing on the target branch. Risk boundary: uncoordinated cherry-picks cause duplicate commits and merge surprises, so document the plan before picking.
